title: GBE-CAND-018 Rejection or Repair Package
readmeBody: Current GBE-CAND-018 is rejected for deep validation; only a fresh, separately frozen variant may continue later.
scientificQuestion: Should GBE-CAND-018 be deep-validated now, or rejected in its current form because the evidence and lineage do not meet deep-validation preconditions?
limitations: This triage only re-audits existing GBE v1 public artifacts plus bounded internal receipts; it does not execute a new external validation campaign.
The current candidate ID has a provenance conflict between initial candidate generation, death-gate filtering, and later promotion.
The evidence-triad idea may remain worth testing, but it cannot inherit GBE-CAND-018 without a fresh candidate card and freeze.
Holdout and replay evidence from GBE v1 contains failures, partials, and caveats that block direct promotion.
Mechanism pressure was plausible but underpowered and did not eliminate documentation completeness, selection artifact, or popularity baselines.
No outside expert review was performed in this triage.
No external-review-ready, prize-grade, breakthrough, broad intelligence, adoption, legal, medical, wet-lab, or universal truth claim is made.
disclaimer: Bounded GBE-CAND-018 triage only. This package rejects the current candidate for deep validation readiness and does not claim external validation, breakthrough, broad autonomous intelligence, prize-significance, external adoption, or universal truth.
currentClaim: Machine-checkable evidence-triad completeness appears to predict benchmark/protocol reproducibility risk better than headline benchmark popularity in bounded public metadata and replay probes.
